_github_issues.description: GitHub issues explicitly referenced in project tracking material. Only add issues referenced in spreadsheet, reports, Bugzilla bugs, or annotations — do not sweep for new ones. Fetch state on each report run.

37561.title: webrtc/protocol/handover-datachannel.html and webrtc/protocol/handover.html attempt to perform an invalid ICE restart in an answer

2899.title: No way to observe DataChannel-only transport events in initial negotiation
2899.resolution: Spec now requires pc.sctp to be accessible before negotiation completes. Unblocks RTCSctpTransport-events and RTCSctpTransport-maxChannels investigation.

_meta.description: Per-test annotations capturing non-obvious context not derivable from WPT scores or Bugzilla alone. Seed this at the start of each report run so findings persist across sessions.
status_values: passing       — fully passing in Firefox now
ff-bug        — confirmed Firefox bug, fix path known
wpt-flaw      — test is wrong; WPT fix needed, not a Firefox bug
wpt-invalid   — test is invalid per spec
on-hold       — waiting on spec decision or upstream discussion
multi-browser — not FF-only; Chrome or Safari also fail
risky         — fix could break live services; needs telemetry/care
untracked     — FF-only failure not yet in triage spreadsheet or meta bug

webrtc/RTCConfiguration-iceCandidatePool.note: Depends on RTCIceTransport (bug 1307994) landing first — that interface is required to expose the pool. Do not expect this to be fixed before candidate-exchange.

webrtc/RTCConfiguration-rtcpMuxPolicy.ht.note: REOPENED. IETF spec (RFC 8858) added nuance here — Firefox implements negotiated rtcp-mux (the 'require' policy disables this), which the spec technically requires but other browsers never implemented. Fix is safe: change behavior to match the other browsers' interpretation. High value (14 points).

webrtc/RTCDataChannel-GC.html?interop-20.note: GC behavior is not fully understood. The test releases all PC/DC references; our GC has known bugs some of which are not yet diagnosed. Effort is variable — could balloon if we want to write additional tests and chase the mysteries.

webrtc/RTCPeerConnection-iceConnectionSt.note: Triage sheet said 'test is simply wrong — sees ICE go to checking on the way to connected, which is normal'. However, 11/14 subtests now pass (March 27, 2026), so the situation is more nuanced. 3 subtests remain; bug 2019346 is NEW and uninvestigated for those 3.

webrtc/RTCPeerConnection-mandatory-getSt.note: Safari also fails (70/73 as of March 27). Not in the FF-only burndown. Firefox-specific failures (11/73) are stats fields not yet implemented. Nico (1728364) is working on RTCAudioSourceStats. The remaining FF-specific failures are likely subsumed by RTCTransportStats work (1225723).

webrtc/RTCPeerConnection-setLocalDescrip.note: Spreadsheet: 'test is wrong — tests for InvalidStateError but spec now guards with InvalidModificationError, which we correctly reject with'. Not FF-only (Safari also fails 1/7). Bug 2019353 filed to fix the WPT test itself.
PRANSWER support. Triage: 'sounds easy but this is one of those cases where subtle/difficult spec bugs are likely'. No service is known to rely on this feature.

webrtc/RTCPeerConnection-setRemoteDescri.note: Same as setLocalDescription-pranswer. Both tests are gated on the same bug (1004510).

webrtc/RTCRtpParameters-codecs.html?inte.note: Safari also fails 2/18 (as of March 27). Not in FF-only burndown. Most of the FF failures (16) were previously H264 infra — bug 2019375 is now FIXED so true FF compliance is now measurable. Remaining 16 FF failures are likely sdpFmtpLine serialization (bug 1751671). Re-evaluate after next run.

webrtc/RTCRtpParameters-encodings.html?i.note: Multi-browser: Chrome fails 2/13, Safari fails 6/13 (as of March 27). Not FF-only. Firefox failures are from setParameters compat mode that was once needed for simulcast interop. Triage: 'not a good idea right now — if Glean telemetry shows nobody is misusing this API, we could do this'. Check telemetry before acting.
webrtc/RTCRtpParameters-encodings.html?i.risk: Disabling compat mode could break services with Firefox-specific simulcast setParameters code.

webrtc/RTCRtpParameters-rtcp.html.note: Fix this first (trivial) — it is a prerequisite for headerExtensions coverage and RTCRtpReceiver-getParameters. Fixing 1765852 unlocks the full 1765851 cluster.

webrtc/RTCRtpParameters-transactionId.ht.note: Same setParameters compat mode risk as RTCRtpParameters-encodings. Bug 2019370 is the WPT-side disable; 1803388 is the Firefox-side removal. Check Glean telemetry before removing compat mode.
webrtc/RTCRtpParameters-transactionId.ht.risk: Could break services using the old Firefox-specific setParameters simulcast pattern.

webrtc/RTCRtpReceiver-getParameters.html.note: Requires BOTH bug 1765851 (headerExtensions) AND bug 1765852 (rtcp) to be fixed. Fix 1765852 first (trivial); then 1765851.

webrtc/RTCRtpSender-setParameters.html?i.note: setParameters compat mode. Same risk as transactionId and encodings tests.
webrtc/RTCRtpSender-setParameters.html?i.risk: Could break services using old Firefox-specific setParameters simulcast pattern.

webrtc/RTCRtpTransceiver-setCodecPrefere.note: Improved from 0/5 → 4/5 between March 23 and March 27 2026 despite bug 2016190 still being NEW/open. One subtest remains. Investigate what changed — may be related to another landing.

webrtc/RTCSctpTransport-events.html.note: Was on-hold pending spec decision. w3c/webrtc-pc#2899 CLOSED March 12 2026 via PR #3094 (jan-ivar): 'Expose pc.sctp transport in have-(local|remote)-offer'. Spec now settled — pc.sctp should be accessible before negotiation completes. Needs investigation to determine whether the WPT test matches the new spec text, and whether Firefox needs a fix.

webrtc/RTCSctpTransport-maxChannels.html.note: Was on-hold for the same reason as RTCSctpTransport-events. w3c/webrtc-pc#2899 now CLOSED (March 12 2026). Same investigation needed.

webrtc/RTCRtpSender-getStats.https.html.note: FF-only failure: 4/5 as of March 27 2026. Not in triage spreadsheet; tracked via annotations. The failing subtest checks that sender.getStats() returns candidate-pair, local-candidate, and remote-candidate stat entries — Firefox likely does not walk the stats graph far enough to include ICE candidate stats in the sender-scoped report. No existing bug covers RTCIceCandidatePairStats/RTCLocalIceCandidateStats/RTCRemoteIceCandidateStats directly; see also bug 2019331 (RTCTransportStats.selectedCandidatePairId) as related work.

webrtc/rtp-stats-lifetime.https.html?int.note: Triage: 'probably more to it than just the linked bug'. The bug covers outbound-rtp stats availability after offer/answer, but the 3 failing subtests may implicate spec issues too. Needs investigation before estimating effort.

webrtc/RTCError.html?interop-2026.note: Fixed by bug 1814459 (RTCError for SDP parse errors, Anshul). Was 0/20 at triage. Confirmed 20/20 as of March 23 2026.

webrtc/RTCPeerConnectionIceErrorEvent.ht.note: Fixed by bug 1561441 (Anshul). Was 0/1 at triage. Confirmed 1/1 as of March 23 2026.

webrtc/RTCPeerConnection-getStats.https..note: Was 6/7 at triage (different test variant counted). Confirmed 8/8 as of March 23 2026. Note: Safari has 1 failure here (7/8) — not Firefox's problem.

webrtc/protocol/additional-codecs.html.note: Test does not account for the sdpFmtpLine present for video/VP8 in getCapabilities result. Bug 2019387 filed to fix the WPT test. Not a Firefox bug.

webrtc/protocol/bundle.https.html?intero.note: Two separate failures. Bug 1805480 (first failure): probably very easy/safe. Bug 1805484 (second failure, enforce bundle-policy as answerer): niche, might cause breakage — no other browser currently enforces this. Do the first, carefully consider the second.
webrtc/protocol/bundle.https.html?intero.risk: 1805484 could cause SDP negotiation failures for services with misconfigured bundle policies.

webrtc/protocol/crypto-suite.https.html.note: Part of the RTCTransportStats cluster (bug 1225723 + sub-bugs 2019331, 2019333, 2019349, 2019355, 2019356, 2019389). Worth batching all transport stats work together — fixes crypto-suite (6), dtls-setup (4), and transport-stats (2) simultaneously.

webrtc/protocol/dtls-setup.https.html.note: Part of RTCTransportStats cluster. See crypto-suite annotation. 3/7 passing as of March 27 — improvement from 0/4 at triage; test appears to have grown too.

webrtc/protocol/h264-profile-levels.http.note: H264 infra (bug 2019375) is now FIXED as of ~March 2026. The remaining 5/8 FF failures are Firefox bug 2013936: tab crash on SDP with h264 profile_level_id > 5. The crash is the blocker — not infra.

webrtc/protocol/handover-datachannel.htm.note: Test is broken (WPT issue #37561). Bug 2019461 filed to fix or remove it from WPT. Not a Firefox bug.

webrtc/protocol/handover.html.note: Same broken test issue as handover-datachannel.html. WPT issue #37561.

webrtc/protocol/rtp-demuxing.html.note: Was an H264 infra failure (0/1 at triage, 1/2 March 23). Now 2/2 — fixed by bug 2019375 (H264 plugin). Test also grew from 1 to 2 subtests.

webrtc/protocol/rtp-headerextensions.htm.note: 2/6 passing as of March 27. Bug 2019469: failure due to recvonly extmap not applying to other side's recvonly — this may be a test issue. Bug 1340372 (Daniel): extmap-allow-mixed support and 2-byte extension IDs. Also: test grew from 4 to 6 subtests.

webrtc/protocol/codecs-subsequent-offer..note: Was H264 infra failure (0/1 at triage). Now 1/1 — fixed by bug 2019375 (H264 plugin).

webrtc/protocol/video-codecs.https.html.note: Was H264 infra failure (0/2 at triage, 1/3 March 23). Now 3/3 — fixed by bug 2019375 (H264 plugin). Test also grew from 2 to 3 subtests.

webrtc/protocol/transceiver-mline-recycl.note: FF-only failure: 0/2 as of March 27 2026. Not in triage spreadsheet; tracked via annotations. Both subtests fail: Firefox does not recycle rejected (port=0) m-line slots for new transceivers per JSEP §5.2.2 — it appends new m-lines instead. Covers local negotiation (audio→audio, audio→video, video→video recycling) and remote negotiation (pre-rejected remote m-line reuse). Pure JSEP/SDP compliance issue; no media flow required.

webrtc/protocol/ice-ufragpwd.html.note: Bug 1617686 (Anshul Malik) RESOLVED/FIXED. Score confirmed 2/2 in WPT run 066334c420 (March 28 2026). Was 0/2 on March 27 due to run timing lag — fix landed after that run was captured.

webrtc/simulcast/h264.https.html.note: Was H264 infra failure. Fixed by bug 2019375 (apehrson, RESOLVED/FIXED ~March 2026).

webrtc/simulcast/negotiation-encodings.h.note: Bare path deprecated Feb 24 2026 by WPT commit fedcf6f7c2 (bug 2016479, authored by jib). Test split into ?interop-2026 and ?rest variants. Always use the ?interop-2026 variant for tracking. Current ?interop-2026 scores (March 27 2026): FF=4/5, CR=1/5, SA=5/5. Chrome failing 4/5 confirms this is a WPT test flaw — duplicated extmap IDs in rewritten SDP (ridToMid/midToRid issue). Not a Firefox bug. No standalone bug filed.

webrtc/simulcast/rid-manipulation.html.note: Test is invalid per RFC 8853 §5.3.2-4. WPT issue #37564 open. Not a Firefox bug — Firefox correctly rejects this.
